items.name: Combat Boots (Broken In)
items.description: Two pairs of well-broken-in combat boots
items.whyNeeded: Selection involves ruck marches and extended field movement. Blisters are a common reason candidates withdraw.
items.selectionTips: Break both pairs in over 6+ months. Rotate daily to allow drying.

recommendedProducts.name: Garmont T8 NFS Tactical Boot
recommendedProducts.reason: Fast break-in period and excellent breathability for Fort Liberty's climate. Lightweight enough for the mixed physical and classroom demands of CA selection.

items.name: Rucksack (65L+)
items.description: Large frame rucksack for ruck marches
items.whyNeeded: Ruck marches are a key physical evaluation component of CA A&S.
items.selectionTips: Break in your ruck before reporting. Hip belt fit is the most important factor.

recommendedProducts.name: ALICE Large Field Pack with Frame
recommendedProducts.reason: External aluminum frame handles heavy ruck weights and distributes load for long-distance movement during selection events.

items.name: OCP Uniforms
items.whyNeeded: Worn throughout the selection process. Expect field conditions.
items.selectionTips: Bring at least four sets. Laundry access may be limited during field phases.

items.name: Lensatic Compass
items.description: Military lensatic compass for land navigation
items.whyNeeded: Land navigation is an evaluated skill during CA A&S.
items.selectionTips: Practice regularly before the course. Know how to navigate day and night.

recommendedProducts.name: Cammenga 3H Tritium Lensatic Compass
recommendedProducts.reason: Military-standard tritium compass — reliable in all conditions. Accurate to 1° for land navigation courses at Fort Liberty.

items.name: Hydration System
items.whyNeeded: North Carolina heat and humidity make continuous hydration essential during physical events.
items.selectionTips: CamelBak or similar. Keep it filled and drink regularly.

recommendedProducts.name: CamelBak BFM 3L Hydration Pack
recommendedProducts.reason: Hands-free 3L capacity keeps you hydrated through long selection events without stopping. Molle-compatible for integration with your ruck setup.

items.name: Wool Socks (Quality)
items.whyNeeded: Blister prevention is critical during ruck marches and field movement.
items.selectionTips: Darn Tough or Smartwool. Bring at least 8 pairs and change frequently.

recommendedProducts.name: Darn Tough Boot Full Cushion Sock
recommendedProducts.reason: Merino wool regulates temperature in Fort Liberty's variable climate. Full cushion protects feet during high-mileage selection events.

items.name: Headlamp (Red Filter)
items.selectionTips: Useful for night land navigation and field exercises. Red filter preserves night vision.

recommendedProducts.name: Princeton Tec Remix Pro Headlamp
recommendedProducts.reason: Multiple brightness modes and hundreds of hours on standard AAs. Trusted by SOF units for field reliability.

items.name: Electrolyte Supplements
items.selectionTips: Fort Liberty heat causes dangerous cramping. Supplement daily during physical events.

recommendedProducts.name: Liquid IV Hydration Multiplier
recommendedProducts.reason: Clinically proven 2–3× faster hydration than water alone. Prevents the cramping that forces candidates out of selection.

items.name: Notebooks and Pens
items.selectionTips: Civil Affairs involves detailed note-taking during exercises. Rite in the Rain recommended.

recommendedProducts.name: Rite in the Rain All-Weather Notebook
recommendedProducts.reason: Waterproof pages survive field conditions. Essential for documenting civil reconnaissance and exercise scenarios.

items.name: GPS Devices
items.notes: All navigation is map and compass only during selection events.

items.name: Pre-Workout Stimulants
items.notes: Risk of cardiac events under extreme physical stress. Not authorized.
